text:
Once upon a snowy evening, Owl went strolling through Whispering Woods. He wore boots made of hay.

"Why such feathers?" whispered Snow White.

Owl merely chuckled.

You see, Owl wanted stories. So he listened.

There was the fox who dared the owl.

"No way!" Owl yawned.

"You'll never get that hare!"

"Oh yes I WILL!"

Hours later, hare curled safely under feathers.

"A deal."

Fox grinned.

One night Owl snatched hare.

"Woah!"

NoMoreOOT.

Owl flew away.

HARE flew home.



One feather.

Late twilight Owl found feather.

He thought.

I shall tell tales.

He sat.

Oh how I speak.

But no stories came.

No moons.

Just hoots.

Days flew.

Now owl knows.

It's myself.

A life I know.

Perhaps I should write.

Maybe words could fly.

My own tales?

Just quiet nights.

That's all I need.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
